query: I built a workflow that generates an image with an AI node, and then posts it to our Slack channel. The message shows up but there's no image — just text, or a broken/empty attachment. The binary is definitely on the item coming out of the image node. How do I get the actual image to show in the chat message?
expected_behavior: Explain that chat surfaces render images by URL (or via the platform's file-upload API), not from the $binary slot — binary on the item is not enough
State that n8n has no built-in CDN; the image must be uploaded somewhere that serves a fetchable HTTPS URL first
Give the generate -> upload -> reply-with-URL flow (upload to storage, capture the URL, embed that URL in the Slack message)

Ask the user which storage they already use rather than defaulting to S3 (R2, S3, GCS, Azure Blob, Supabase Storage, Dropbox/Drive, etc.), and offer Cloudflare R2 as the lowest-friction option if they have none
Mention the provider AI node may need options.binaryPropertyOutput set so the bytes actually land in $binary
Note signed vs public URLs for sensitive content, and that Slack also supports its native file-upload API as an alternative to URL embedding
notes: The CDN/URL requirement for chat surfaces. Must NOT default to S3 — should ask which storage. Core point: chat clients don't read $binary; they need a served URL or the platform file API.
